item.title: drawing decisions
item.description: This is a photo I took about three years ago of the Moon using a digital camera with a slow shutter speed. The camera acted as a sort of a paintbrush, where I moved the light from the Moon that came into contact with the camera lens like ink along a blank page. The shutter lasted about two seconds which I set because I wanted time to ‘draw with the Moon’ using the camera but didn’t want to let too much light in and loose the contrast of the bright moon against the night sky.

item.title: starfish.systems
item.description: ::starfish.systems activated :: A tiny, experimental design studio exploring the realms of spacial interfaces, virtual spaces and the transference of visual information through digital applications.

item.title: Black Hole
item.description: Each dot = 50,000 data records collected by ‘Black Hole’ GCHQ program each day. (This includes: search history, social media posts and messages, call logs, emails and more) Coptic book binding by hand.

item.title: Election by Communication
item.description: drawingmachine.py Python program that draws based on live tweets on the 2016 US election depending on specific keywords. The bottom image depicts just a snapshot of the constant stream of content that was being used to draw these shapes.

item.title: Elevator pitch
item.description: Self-published zine on non-verbal communication and anxiety.

item.title: Exhibitions/Residencies/Publications
item.description: Joya: Artist in Residence (2022) University College London - Multimedia Anthropology Lab collaborative exhibition (virtual) - uclmal.com/exhibition ‘Open Fragments’ - Pragamata Collective (online) ‘Gaps between gaps’ - Solo Exhibition Stallan-Brand Architects (Glasgow) ‘Fantôme/Aave’ - Vaapan Taiteen Tila (Helsinki)/Galerie au Medicis (Paris) ‘How to Survive Kaamos’ exhibition at Vapaan Taiteen Tila (Helsinki) ‘Rooted’ - Where People Sleep (Glasgow) ‘Sobremesa’ - Wasps Studios (Glasgow) ‘More is Less’ - Laurieston Arches (Glasgow) ‘Feedback Loop’ - GSA Photography studio

item.title: Fantôme/Aave
item.description: ‘This is Fiction’ - Single Channel Video Projection (First Image) In collaboration with Mensonge Collectif, co-curated with Cesar Kaci and Aliha Thalien Vapaan Taiteen Tila Helsinki/Galerie au Medicis Helsinki - 2019 Link to full video

item.title: Gaps between gaps
item.description: Stallan-Brand (Glasgow) March 12 - April 3 2020 An exhibition on conspiracy and world-building.

item.title: Generated glitch model paintings
item.description: Generated, Glitch, Model (GGM) Paintings - 3D Digital Render - Ongoing Experimenting with a visual medium developed using a variety of online AI text generators, picture editing phone apps and Maya software which I named Generated, Glitch, Model (GGM) paintings. blue systems, 2022 spacing, 2022

item.title: Generated Worlds
item.description: Python script that generates random terms for world building - making use of terms from politics, philosophy, ecology, computing, psychology and geography.

item.title: Info
item.description: If you’re looking for a creative generalist with a stack of design, tech and some marketing skills then reach out to me. I’m particularly interested in working with independent galleries, small press publishers, digital product developers, web3 organisations/businesses, fashion brands and record labels. Currently working at BookMachine, a small design and marketing agency in London, building digital products, automating workflows and building a company Operating System. Elsewhere, I’m a digital designer, artist and writer working on my own series of projects including building starfish.

item.title: Keywords - Poster Series
item.description: A series of five posters on leaked keywords used in NSA Surveillance software.

item.title: Pretend Ai, Pretending to be People
item.description: Collaborative exhibition with UCL’s Multimedia Anthropology Lab (2021) This virtual space presents an ongoing conversation and collaboration between artists Zach Mason and Joseph Clark both who are interested in data collection, visualisation and the transformations of artificial intelligence. In a bedroom, inspired by the setting that many conversations between us have taken place, we will take on the role of AI where we limit ourselves to responding only to what has been said through the information present in the room, without the context of each others prior intentions or personal lives.

item.title: Starfish
item.description: Self-published zine - 2021 Starfish Press began as a continuation to the publication of ‘Starfish’ in 2021. Still in the process of being established, the aim of Starfish is to create visual work which encounters new ideas that bridge print and digital culture. Co-editor and photographer - Annie Joy Boothroyd
